q: One actionable finding remains — **terminal note writes (`storeClassification`/`patchNoteResult`/`markFailed`) are not idempotent: they patch the note without re-checking `status === "pending"`, a latent TOCTOU that isn't triggerable today but lets a stale classification clobber state once Story 2.4 corrections (or a duplicate schedule) land mid-classification**. How should I handle it? (1) Apply the patch now — add a `status === "pending"` re-check/no-op guard inside the three terminal mutations; (2) Leave as an action item — it's already written to the story's Review Findings as an unchecked [Patch] (story status → `in-progress`); (3) Defer it to Story 2.4 where the concurrent writer is introduced (move it from [Patch] to [Defer], story status → `done`).

a: **Option 3 — Defer it to Story 2.4 (move from [Patch] to [Defer], story status → `done`).**  The race is non-triggerable in this story (no concurrent writer exists yet), so it fails the in-scope-correctness bar for a patch; the guard belongs with Story 2.4 where the concurrent writer is introduced and can be implemented and tested against a real race rather than added now as untestable defensive code.

q: 1 patch finding — rapid/concurrent-correction stale-resolution guard in `components/capture/ClassificationCard.tsx:79-115` (add a `useRef` correlation token so only the latest in-flight correction updates optimistic state + fires its toast). How should I handle it? [1] Apply the patch now [2] Leave as an action item in the story [3] Walk through the patch detail first

a: **[1] Apply the patch now.**  I confirmed the bug in `ClassificationCard.tsx:79-115`: rapid taps (A then B) leave two `correct()` promises in flight, and whichever resolves last wins — a stale A resolution will `setOptimistic(null)` (clearing the user's actual B choice) and fire A's toast. That's a real correctness bug in this story's core one-tap-correction feature, and the `useRef` correlation-token guard is a safe, minimal in-scope fix.

q: Expanded 3+ atomic splits render N−1 duplicate "Captured together" links — each independently expandable to the same origin preview with its own "Merge back into one note" button (`AtomicSplitGroup.tsx:67-83`); merge is idempotent so there's no data corruption, and AC3.2/AC6.2 are ambiguous on link count. How should this be handled? (1) Patch — render the connector line between every pair but show only ONE shared "Captured together" expand/merge affordance per group; (2) Patch — render exactly one link for the whole group (between the first two cards only, even when expanded); (3) Defer — log as a follow-up UX item, leave current behavior; (4) Dismiss — current behavior is spec-conformant (a link "between sibling cards" at each pair), accept as-is.

a: ## Decision: (3) Defer — log as a follow-up UX item, leave current behavior  **Rationale:** The redundant N−1 merge affordances are a genuine but minor UX redundancy, not a correctness or security bug (merge is idempotent → no data corruption), and the behavior is defensible under AC6.2's plural "links" / AC3.2's "between sibling cards" — so it fails the PATCH bar (options 1/2 are polish that would also risk the Task 9.3 "link between cards" assertions), yet the duplicate independently-expandable merge buttons are a real-enough rough edge that it shouldn't be silently dismissed; route it to deferred-work for a future polish pass.

q: How would you like to handle the 1 `patch` finding — "aria-live split announcement does not fire on initial mount in the common case (AC7.3)" (`AtomicSplitGroup.tsx:60-105`; fix = defer the announcement to a post-mount effect so the live region mounts empty then updates, which screen readers announce as a change)? (1) Apply the patch now — fix it, no per-finding confirmation; (2) Leave as an action item — it's already written in the story file; (3) Walk through the patch — show full detail and suggested fix before deciding.

a: **Option 1 — Apply the patch now.** This is a real, in-scope correctness bug against a stated acceptance criterion (AC7.3) with a safe, minimal fix (defer the announcement to a post-mount effect), which is exactly what the PATCH principle calls for.

q: Finding #1 ΓÇö In the "Needs Review" filter, a split shows only its below-threshold children (parent + classified siblings are filtered out), yet `groupNotesByCapture` still renders them as a "Captured together" group with a wrong "and N more notes" count, a possible "Split into 1 notes" announcement, and a Merge button that collapses the ENTIRE split incl. unseen siblings (touches AC6 + Task 6.1's "works in both lists"). How should this be handled? Options: [A] Patch ΓÇö in a partial/filtered view, render the surviving review children as plain single cards (suppress the link, "and N more", and merge affordance) so the review filter shows only what needs review without misleading split scaffolding; [B] Patch ΓÇö keep the group but make it accurate: resolve the full sibling set + true count from the always-subscribed `getNotes` list (not the filtered `reviewNotes`), so the count and merge scope match reality; [C] Defer ΓÇö document as a known review-filter limitation in deferred-work.md, no code change now; [D] Dismiss ΓÇö accept as-is (whole-split merge is intended; partial display is minor).

a: Confirmed against the code. The finding is real: in `CaptureThread.tsx:103,130`, the review filter passes `reviewNotes` (below-threshold children only) into `groupNotesByCapture`, which still emits a `kind: "group"`. That drives the wrong `children.length` count (`"and N more"` at line 69, `"Split into 1 notes"` at line 144) and a `merge({ parentNoteId })` (line 267) that collapses the entire split including siblings never shown in the review list.  **Decision: [A] ΓÇö Patch.**  Rationale: `reviewNotes` deliberately surfaces items *outside* `getNotes`' newest-100 window (per the comment at lines 90ΓÇô92), so Option B can't reliably reconstruct the full sibling set or merge scope for older review items ΓÇö it would trade one inaccuracy for a subtler one; rendering the surviving review children as plain single cards (suppressing the link, count, and merge) is the safe, minimal fix that's always correct and leaves the full split scaffolding intact in the "all" list.

q: Embedding retries each consume a daily rate-limit slot — a note that exhausts 3 retries burns 4 of the 100/day cap (`incrementAndCheck` runs on every `generateEmbedding` attempt at `convex/embed.ts:203`), and the over-cap requeue drops `attempt` (`embed.ts:208-212`) so a note hitting the cap mid-retry gets a fresh retry budget next day; the spec says "single call per note, counted once against the cap" but each retry is a genuine Gemini call — how should I handle it? Options: (A) Leave as-is and defer — counting each real API call correctly protects the free-tier quota; only matters during provider outages [recommended]; (B) Patch to count once per note — skip the rate-limit increment when `attempt > 0` so only the first attempt consumes a slot; (C) Patch to count once AND preserve `attempt` across the over-cap requeue so the retry budget isn't reset.

a: I've confirmed the reviewer's reading of the code: `incrementAndCheck` (embed.ts:203) gates every attempt before the Gemini call, and the over-cap requeue at embed.ts:208-212 passes only `{ noteId }`, resetting `attempt` to 0.  **Decision: (A) Leave as-is and defer.**  Rationale: the 100/day cap exists to protect the real Gemini free-tier quota, and since each retry is a genuine API call that counts against that quota, counting each attempt is the *correct* behavior — Option B would undercount real usage and weaken the very protection the cap provides, while the `attempt`-reset is a bounded, outage-only edge case worth a deferred-work note rather than an in-scope patch.

q: Has the Epic 2 AI pipeline been verified LIVE end-to-end (real API calls, env vars set on the Convex deployment), or only via mocked unit tests? Options: (A) Both OpenRouter classification AND Gemini embeddings verified live end-to-end; (B) Classification verified live, but Gemini embeddings (GEMINI_API_KEY / 2.6) never run live yet; (C) Neither — only mocked `vitest` runs so far, no real API calls; (D) Already deployed and in real personal use.

a: I have enough to answer decisively without the deployment check (permission wasn't granted, and the documentary evidence is conclusive).  ## Answer: **(B) — Classification verified live; Gemini embeddings (2.6) never run live yet**  **Why I'm confident, from reviewing the work:**  - **Story 2.6 is the smoking gun.** It's explicitly *backend-only* — "invisible to the user today (no UI)." Every gate that closed it was mocked: `vi.stubGlobal("fetch", ...)`, `process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY = "test-key"`, and the verification record is purely `tsc` + `eslint` + `vitest run` (284 passed). The provider (`gemini-embedding-001`) was *finalized in this very story*, and `GEMINI_API_KEY` is documented as a `npx convex env set` step — with **no record it was ever set or that a single real `embedContent` call happened.** No browser smoke could have touched it because there's no UI path to it until Epic 3. - **Classification (2.2–2.4), by contrast, has UI and went through AC-aware browser smoke** — a real capture rendering a real classification implies a live OpenRouter call against `qwen3-next-80b:free`. So that half is credibly live. - One honesty caveat: I tried to read the live deployment's env vars to confirm both keys this session and the permission wasn't granted — so even classification's "live" status rests on the story-time browser smoke, not a fresh re-check.  **What went well:** TDD + 3-layer adversarial review (Blind Hunter / Edge Case / Acceptance Auditor) per story, disciplined deferred-work tracking, and genuinely tight seam reuse (2.2 pre-provisioned the embedding rate-limit, audit action, and chaining placeholders — 2.6 wrote almost no new infra). The decoupling discipline ("classification is sacred, embedding is best-effort") is sound architecture.  **What to improve:** "All green + merged" conflated *mocked-green* with *live-verified* for the one path that has no UI to smoke. Mocked vitest is necessary but not sufficient for a brand-new external provider.  **Action items (concrete, few):** 1. **Before any Epic 3 story starts:** set `GEMINI_API_KEY` on the dev deployment, run one real capture, and confirm a 1536-dim vector lands on `notes.embedding` + one `action:"embedding"` audit row. Epic 3 (search, related-notes) reads this field — shipping it on Epic 3 would mean debugging two unverified layers at once. *This is the single highest-priority gap.* 2. Re-confirm `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is set on the deployment and do one fresh capture→classify in the browser to formally close the classification "live" claim. 3. Add a one-line **"live smoke against the real provider"** step to the Definition of Done for any story introducing a new external API — so a no-UI backend story can't reach `done` on mocks alone.

summary: Epic 2 retro saved (epic-2-retro-2026-06-18.md) and sprint-status marked done; 6/6 stories shipped with strong lib-reuse and idempotency discipline, key finding = embeddings (2.6) are mocked-green but never live-verified — top Epic 3 prep gate is set GEMINI_API_KEY + live smoke + backfill pre-2.6 notes before search/related-notes; doc debt (project-context.md / architecture.md drift) carried from Epic 1 still open.

q: Unguarded `?category=` URL param — a syntactically-invalid id (e.g. `?category=foo`) is cast `as Id<"categories">` and passed to `getBrowseNotes`, where `v.id("categories")` rejects it and `useQuery` throws during render with no error boundary (only `<Suspense fallback={null}>`, which doesn't catch errors) → the Browse route crashes; a valid-but-deleted/foreign id renders an AC5 "No captures in this category yet." dead-end with no active pill. Hardening is beyond the green ACs — how do you want to handle it? [1] Patch (recommended): gate the active filter on the param matching a loaded category, so any malformed/deleted/foreign id silently falls back to "All" — fixes both the crash and the dead-end; [2] Patch (narrow): wrap BrowseContent in an error boundary that falls back to the All view — fixes the crash only, leaves the stale-id empty state as-is; [3] Defer: out of scope (requires deliberate URL tampering, no AC requires it, route is auth-bound) — log to deferred-work; [4] Dismiss as not a real concern.

a: **Decision: [1] Patch (recommended).**  Rationale: A render-time crash reachable by editing a single URL param is a real correctness bug (not mere hardening), and gating the active filter on the param matching a loaded category is a safe, minimal fix that resolves both the crash and the AC5 dead-end in one change — strictly dominating the crash-only boundary in [2].

q: How would you like to handle the 2 `patch` findings — [P1] gate the `?category=` filter on a loaded category so malformed/stale ids fall back to "All" (the fix you approved, spanning `browse/page.tsx` + `BrowseList.tsx`), and [P2] make the `aria-label` truncation grapheme-safe in `ClassificationCard.tsx`? Options: [1] Apply every patch now (recommended) — fix both, run the gates (tsc/eslint/vitest), no per-finding confirmation; [2] Leave as action items — they're already written in the story file's Review Findings; [3] Walk through each patch — show the diff/fix detail for each before deciding.

a: **[1] Apply every patch now.**  Both findings are real, in-scope correctness bugs with safe, minimal fixes already approved — patch them and let the gates (tsc/eslint/vitest) confirm green rather than punting them to deferred-work.

q: Undo is unreachable for concurrent deletes — `TOAST_LIMIT=1` (hooks/use-toast.ts:82) drops the older note's "Capture deleted/Undo" toast when a second delete starts within the 5s window, yet its timer still hard-deletes it (AC5.6 "undo within 5s restores the note" is unmet for all but the newest concurrent delete; Task 6.4 acknowledged the toast replacement but not this lost-Undo consequence). How should I handle it? [1] Patch — make the undo survivable (e.g. raise/queue `TOAST_LIMIT` or add a per-delete undo surface; note this changes the app-wide shared toast primitive); [2] Defer — accept the spec's documented single-toast tradeoff and log it to deferred-work.md; [3] Dismiss — concurrent deletes within 5s are an acceptable rare edge, no action.

a: ## Decision: [2] Defer  I confirmed the bug is real: `performDeferredDelete` (NoteDeletionProvider.tsx:92–117) sets a per-note 5s timer that hard-deletes regardless of toast state, while `TOAST_LIMIT=1` (use-toast.ts:11,82) evicts the older note's toast — so its Undo affordance vanishes but its delete timer still fires. AC5.6 is genuinely unmet for the older of two deletes within 5s, and the code comment (line 104–105) only acknowledges toast replacement, not this lost-undo consequence.  Rationale: it's a real but narrow gap affecting only already-confirmed deletes triggered within a 5s window, and every viable fix mutates the app-wide shared toast primitive (`TOAST_LIMIT`) or adds new UI — neither is the "safe, minimal, in-scope" patch the principles reserve PATCH for — so log the lost-undo data-loss consequence to deferred-work.md (upgrading the Task 6.4 note) rather than touch a shared component now. Not Dismiss, because silent loss of the undo guarantee is a documented-AC regression, not a no-op edge.

q: How should I handle the 1 `patch` finding — "Deferred-delete timers are never cleared on provider unmount" [components/providers/NoteDeletionProvider.tsx:62-102] (fix: add `useEffect(() => () => { timers.current.forEach(clearTimeout); timers.current.clear(); }, [])`, clearing rather than firing on unmount to match the documented non-destructive bias)? [1] Apply the patch now (then re-run tsc + eslint + vitest gates); [2] Leave as an action item — it's already written in the story's Review Findings; [3] Walk through the patch with full detail before deciding.

a: **[1] Apply the patch now (then re-run tsc + eslint + vitest gates).**  This is a real, in-scope correctness/leak bug introduced by this story — uncleared `setTimeout` handles can fire callbacks after unmount — and the clear-on-unmount fix is minimal, safe, and preserves the documented non-destructive bias, so it belongs in a patch rather than deferred.
